meta.role: Human Resources Department - Quality Control Unit Associate

header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) People Operations & Organizational Development professional who thrives on solving problems through data analysis, improving HR operations grounded in evidence, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Always seeking to improve people experience and operational excellence through data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in data analytics to audit 300+ confidential leave cases, using evidence-based insights to refute cost assumptions
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Analyze interview data to identify decision-making ambiguity and cross-functional process gaps
Manage end-to-end lifecycles for 20+ projects, using feedback analysis to optimize operations and vendor performance
Develop process documentation, resource libraries, and workflows for 70+ staff, cutting onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate listening sessions with 25+ stakeholders, surfacing gaps to co-design 3 tailored programs
Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, improving understanding and driving a 78% enrollment rate
Analyzed 2,000+ employee survey responses, surfacing engagement drivers to recommend C-suite actions
Led manager effectiveness initiative, translating data into an executive-ready presentation for training strategy
Managed SharePoint dashboards for people metrics, NPS, and survey insights to drive employee recognition
Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects in surveys, performance review, and L&D
Facilitated organizational change by directing an M&A project and overseeing cross-functional stakeholders
Streamlined onboarding across 3 global sites by clarifying responsibilities and mapping new team workflows
Initiated an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 global locations
Supported 5 HR projects in talent development, succession, and Employee Relations; upheld alignment and confidentiality
Managed HR operations spanning recruitment, compensation analysis, and performance management globally
Coordinated international talent acquisition, including job descriptions, postings, and compensation benchmarking
Drove a 566% application increase in 2 months through personalized global outreach and talent engagement
Planned recruiting events and C-suite panels at top 3 universities, engaging 230+ students and 80+ applicants

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

education.degree: BA in Psychology and Education Studies (GPA: 4.00)
bullets: Relevant Coursework: Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Psychological Testing, Learning and Motivation

projects: Consulting Projects: Enhanced Elanco Animal Health employer branding via survey design
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping employer attractiveness for YouTube
Scholarships: Freeman Asian Scholarship (full tuition), Tuck Bridge Scholarship (full)
skills: HR Systems: HRIS (SAP, Rippling, ADP) · ATS (Greenhouse) · LMS (Canvas, Moodle)
Data & PM Tools: Excel, Google Sheets, SPSS · Project Management (Asana, Airtable) · CRM (HubSpot)
Productivity: Policy/Process Documentation · SharePoint · Notion · Microsoft Office (Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Outlook)
Certifications: HRCI Human Resource Associate Certificate (in progress)

jd.meta: Bank of China - Human Resources Department, Quality Control Unit Associate - New York, NY

changeLog: Changed broad identity noun to People Operations for HR department fit while preserving Jamie's personal opener and three-pillar summary.
Reordered ODN around HR leave-case audit, policy redesign, and process-risk diagnosis.
Selected InGenius bullets for vendor management, operations, documentation, needs assessment, and training.
Selected Vestas bullets for M&A employee records, process gaps, employment-law confidentiality, and strategic HR support.
Added JD-aware skills for policy/process documentation, Excel/data, HR systems, and Microsoft Office without inventing banking tools.
